parameters.prompt: Original photorealistic multiview concept of one isolated upper receiver and accelerator spine for a fictional vacuum-rated induction carbine, exactly 0.30 m long, 0.078 m wide and 0.07 m high. Tapered hexagonal structural shell, sealed breech volume, lower-receiver lug, rear stock socket, forward handguard flange, internal barrel socket, low optic datum, charging actuator and service covers. Distinct non-AR hard-surface design. No barrel, handguard, lower receiver, grip, trigger, magazine, stock, optic, muzzle, ammunition, text, logo, stand, ground or background.

strengths: three views agree on a tapered hexagonal receiver shell and circular accelerator socket
rear coupling, lower mounting lug and forward interface remain readable
the silhouette is distinct from the rejected monolithic current-day rifle study
required_cleanup: remove the small generated pseudo-marking on the side service plate
replace the rail-like top teeth with the authored low-profile optic datum
fit all final interfaces to component-plan sockets rather than generated openings
